summary: Designs a read-only repository review skill with bounded inputs, explicit evidence tags, and no write or network dependency. [CONFIG]

frontmatter.name: repository-risk-review
frontmatter.description: Use when a user asks for a deterministic repository risk review that reads local files, identifies maintainability and safety issues, and returns evidence-tagged findings without editing files.

guiding_principle: The skill must preserve read-only review behavior, ground every finding in inspected files, and avoid speculative remediation claims. [DOC]

procedure.output: Candidate files grouped by code, tests, configuration, and documentation.

procedure.input: Only files selected from the bounded scope.
procedure.output: Observed contracts, ownership boundaries, and validation entry points.

procedure.input: Errors, TODOs, stale comments, weak tests, and risky configuration.
procedure.output: Evidence-backed issue candidates with file references.

procedure.input: Issue candidates and existing project conventions.
procedure.output: Filtered findings that avoid style-only or unsupported claims.
Review report with severity, evidence, and open questions.
Final response that states reviewed scope and test gaps.

quality_criteria: Every finding cites a concrete file or explicitly states that no supporting file was found. [CODE]
Severity labels are based on behavior, data integrity, security, or user-visible risk. [INFERENCE]
The report separates confirmed defects from assumptions and open questions. [CONFIG]
No remediation is claimed as applied because the tool profile is read-only. [CONFIG]
The response names validation gaps without inventing command output. [CODE]
anti_patterns: Editing files while operating under a read-only skill profile.
Listing broad best-practice advice without file-backed evidence.
Treating absence of tests as proof of a defect without behavioral support.
Mixing unrelated repositories or user-private context into the review.

edge_cases.case: The scoped path contains only documentation.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Report documentation-only scope and avoid code-risk claims. [CODE]

edge_cases.case: The user asks for fixes after requesting a review.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: State that this skill produces review output and ask for a separate edit workflow if changes are required. [CONFIG]

edge_cases.case: A suspected issue depends on unavailable runtime data.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Classify the item as an assumption or open question instead of a confirmed defect. [ASSUMPTION]

tool_rationale.reason: Reads bounded local files for evidence. [CODE]
Discovers candidate files inside the user-approved scope. [CODE]
Finds deterministic patterns such as TODOs, test names, and risky configuration keys. [CODE]

validation: The fixture is accepted by validate_design_skill_spec.py without network access. [CODE]
Tool profile and allowed tools are exactly aligned. [CONFIG]
risks: The skill can miss runtime-only failures because it intentionally avoids execution tools. [ASSUMPTION]
